rows.notes: Clear centred page heading at top of page. No amounts in the row; text is fully legible.
Vertical ruling lines easy to align; pounds = 3, shillings = 16, pence = 8 clearly written. Description starts with 'Constabl' (could be 'Constable') but reading is unambiguous from handwriting.
rows.english_description: Payment to the constable for the year 1730 (constable's annual fee).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the constable for the year 1730, amounting to £3 16s 8d.

rows.notes: Numbers align perfectly with the three right-hand columns: 4 | 12 | 4. The Latin label 'Coll: Omnium Animarum' is clearly readable.
rows.english_description: Collection paid for All Souls (a church/feast collection).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Collection paid for All Souls, amounting to £4 12s 4d.

rows.notes: Amount reads 0 | 14 | 6 clearly. The description appears as 'Mayors Capons' in the handwriting; the first word is clear but the second could be an old spelling (e.g. 'capons' or 'copes'), so moderate minor ambiguity in the exact wording but amount is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Purchase or gift of capons for the mayor (food provision).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase or gift of capons for the mayor, amounting to £0 14s 6d.

rows.notes: Amount 0 | 18 | 0 is clearly aligned. Description read as 'Coll: Fidis Christi' (Latin collection for 'Fidis/Feis Christi'); letters are legible though old orthography may vary.
rows.english_description: Collection paid for the Feast of the Faith of Christ (ecclesiastical collection).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Collection paid for the Feast of the Faith of Christ, amounting to £0 18s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount columns align to 1 | 13 | 4. Description interpreted as 'Curia Rogationis pro 20 Annis' — several letters (especially first word) are slightly stylised but phrase is consistent with Latin ledger terms.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Rogation court covering twenty years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rogation court covering twenty years, amounting to £1 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Amount 1 | 19 | 0 is clear. Description is partially faded/abbreviated: I read 'Poorer.' then an abbreviation and parish names 'Newington & Merton' — the parish names are legible but the mid-abbreviation is uncertain (transcribed as 'Exd').
rows.english_description: Alms or poor relief disbursement for Newington and Merton.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Alms or poor relief disbursement for Newington and Merton, amounting to £1 19s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 1 | 14 | 8 aligned with ruling lines. Description reads 'Sanctori -' (final dash visible). Some letters slightly faint but overall readable.
rows.english_description: Payment to the sacrist (church officer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the sacrist, amounting to £1 14s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount 1 | 10 | 4 is clear. The description is brief and the second letter is a little indistinct; transcribed as 'Cogne -' following the visible stroke pattern but alternative readings (e.g. 'Conge') are possible.
rows.english_description: Payment to the cook (kitchen staff).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the cook, amounting to £1 10s 4d.

rows.notes: Numeric columns show 2 | 0 | 0 clearly for this line. The description reads as 'Latrice -' (or possibly 'Latrini'/'Latrice'); the handwriting is faded and the exact Latin/vernacular word is uncertain, so description confidence is moderate.
rows.english_description: Payment to the laundress (washing staff).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the laundress, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 0 | 13 | 4 is aligned and legible. Description appears to be 'Pension' or 'Penson' with a dash; the handwriting of the vowel is slightly unclear but context suggests a pension-related entry.
rows.english_description: Pension payment (retirement allowance or annuity).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Pension payment, amounting to £0 13s 4d.

rows.notes: Shillings and pence read ~13 | 7 (0 | 13 | 7). The word looks like 'Bibliothecarii' (librarian/library); some letters are cramped and the final pence digit is slightly smudged, so there is moderate ambiguity on the exact spelling of the description but numeric columns align.
rows.english_description: Payment to the librarian.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the librarian, amounting to £0 13s 7d.

rows.notes: Amount reads 1 | 3 | 8 (pounds/shillings/pence) though the middle column digits are small. Description appears to be 'Bedle of Beggars' (beadle), handwriting a little cramped; alternative minor spellings possible.
rows.english_description: Payment by the beadle for distribution to beggars (alms overseen by the beadle).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment by the beadle for distribution to beggars, amounting to £1 3s 8d.

rows.notes: Clear amount 4 | 0 | 0. The entry word reads 'Supervision' (or possibly 'Supervisor'); letters are darker and alignment is certain.
rows.english_description: Supervision expenses (administrative oversight or supervisory costs).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Supervision expenses, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 1 | 0 | 0 is unambiguous. Description 'Easter Sermon' is clearly written.
rows.english_description: Payment for the Easter sermon (fee to the preacher).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for the Easter sermon, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount shows 1 | 0 | 0. The description is abbreviated 'Conser. Sermon' (handwriting slightly abbreviated/unclear); I transcribed as seen but exact expansion of 'Conser.' is uncertain.
rows.english_description: Payment for the consecration sermon (fee to the preacher for the consecration).

rows.description: Curate of S. Newington a year & half

rows.notes: Amount 0 | 15 | 0 visible and aligned. Description reads 'Pauperboys' (some old spelling) and is reasonably legible.
